sources.name: Federal Highway Administration — National Scenic Byways

paragraphs: Prior to the development of the Great Smoky Mountains National Park, Newfound Gap was an undiscovered pass two miles east of what was long thought to be the lowest mountain pass over the Great Smoky Mountains, Indian Gap. Indian Gap Road, an unpaved, arduous trail frequented by traders, farmers, and even by the Confederate Army during the American Civil War, was completed in 1839 and named after the old Cherokee Indian trail that the road paralleled. Newfound Gap was not recognized as the lowest gap over the mountains until 1872, when Arnold Guyot measured many of the mountains in the area and determined the "Newfound Gap" to be a lower, more accessible mountain pass.

gallery.desc: The Appalachian Trail continues on towards its Southern terminus in Northern Georgia here, at Newfound Gap , on the border of Tennessee and North Carolina. This is also the starting point for several popular day hikes in the Great Smoky Mountains National Park . Photo taken by myself, Scott Basford, on April 20, 2006.

gallery.cap: Leaf Colors at Newfound Gap NPS1
gallery.desc: NPS image of the forest canopy near Newfound Gap . The trees in the foreground are deciduous trees known as "northern hardwoods," the leaves of which reflect the colors of autumn. As elevation increases in the distance, the region's two chief high-altitude conifer types-- the red spruce and Fraser fir-- become the dominant tree types, as they can bear much colder climatic conditions than the broad

500px provided description: A view of the road to Cherokee, NC as it winds it's way up the Smokey Mountains. Lots of water vapor in the air, it gives the mountains their "Smokey" name [#tn ,#nc ,#smokey mountains ,#newfound gap ,#great smokey mountains national park]

gallery.cap: Fountain, Laura Spelman Rockefeller Memorial, , Great Smoky Mountains
gallery.desc: Constructed between 1938 and 1940, this terraced stone block structure, made of locally quarried stone, was erected by the New Deal-era Civilian Conservation Corps as a memorial to philanthropist Laura Spelman Rockefeller, serving as a focal point of Newfound Gap in the Great Smoky Mountains National Park.
